sections.paragraph:
ὑπόκρισις, εως, ἡ (ὑποκρίνομαι; Hdt.+=‘answer’, then Attic ‘playing a stage role’) in our lit. only in a neg. sense to create a public impression that is at odds with one’s real purposes or motivations, play-acting, pretense, outward show, dissembling (loanword in Engl. ‘hypocrisy’; Polyb. 15, 17, 2; 35, 2, 13; Diod S 1, 76, 1; Appian, Hann. 19 §83, Syr. 61 §319, Mithrid. 14, 48; Ps.-Lucian, Amor. 3; Aesop 284 H.=166 H-H.; 2 Macc 6:25; Ps 4:6; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 43, De Jos. 67; Jos., Bell. 1, 628, Ant. 16, 216; TestBenj 6:5; Hippol, Ref. 9, 11, 13; Did., Gen. 128, 18) Mt 23:28; Mk 12:15; Lk 12:1; Gal 2:13; Js 5:12 v.l. πᾶσαν ὑπόκρισιν; B 19:2; 20:1; 21:4; D 4:12; Hm 8:3. Pl. of the varied forms that pretentious piety assumes 1 Pt 2:1; D 5:1.—ἐν ὑποκρίσει ψευδολόγων misled by smooth talkers 1 Ti 4:2. ἐν ὑποκρίσει insincerely (schol. on Soph., El. 164 p. 111 Papag.) Pol 6:3; Hv 3, 6, 1; m 2:5; Hs 8, 6, 2. Also μεθʼ ὑποκρίσεως 1 Cl 15:1. κατὰ μηδεμίαν ὑπόκρισιν without any pretense at all IMg 3:2 (κατὰ ὑπ. as schol. on Soph., Oed. Col. 1232 p. 451). ἄτερ ὑποκρίσεως without pretense Hs 9, 27, 2 (Leutzsch, Hermas p. 417 n. 338; p. 494 n. 432).—DELG s.v. κρίνω. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

neg. neg. = negative

Engl. Engl. = English

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

Ps.-Lucian Ps.-Lucian , after II a.d.—List 5

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

m m = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

Leutzsch, Leutzsch, Hermas = MLeutzsch, Hirt des Hermas—Lists 1 (s. H), 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1038
